title: canna indica var. nepalensis
intro: Canna indica var. nepalensis is a plant species in the canna family, commonly known as the golden cane or Nepalese canna. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparation of Canna indica var. nepalensis is typically made into a tea, decoction, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
